page: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core performance and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Passmark CPU scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Its memory subsystem delivers exceptional read-only cached bandwidth of 15490.0 GB/sec and uncached write bandwidth of 232.99 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations reaching 296.12 million operations per second. Additionally, the platform demonstrates strong capabilities in large language model (LLM) inference, particularly in prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models.

The r8i.metal-48xl does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options, utilizing the baseline 75 Gbps network bandwidth. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture eliminates virtualization overhead, maximizing the utility of the massive 1.5 TB DDR5 memory pool and 192 vCPUs. This makes it highly resource-dense for enterprise workloads. It is ideal for large-scale in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, heavy multi-threaded compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. It features dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 192 vCPUs, 96 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed, paired with 1536.0 GB of 7 GHz DDR5 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage connectivity. Benchmark data shows top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database execution speeds, including 296.12 million Redis SET operations per second. It also supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. This configuration is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive memory density and high compute throughput without virtualization overhead, making it ideal for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy multi-threaded compilation, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 75 Gbps network baseline, ideal for large-scale databases and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 1.5 TB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server featuring 1.5 TB of DDR5 memory and 192 vCPUs for demanding in-memory database and compute workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz.
1536.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU hardware.
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching, and CPU-based LLM inference.
